retry_reason: The put operation always executes and cannot fail; retrying would attempt a double-put, which is undefined and dangerous.
contract_summary: Put-wrapper that releases a reference on a block_device by decrementing the refcount of its embedded device (via put_device). This corresponds to the release side of a get/put lifecycle (e.g., paired with blkdev_get_by_path or similar). After calling this function, the caller must not access the block_device pointer, as the underlying object may have been freed when the refcount reaches zero. No return value to check; the operation is unconditional.

judge.reason: The function's entire body is a single call to put_device, which decrements the refcount of the block_device's embedded device. There is no other logic, making it a pure put wrapper.
